conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Raphy Varghese is Head of Growth & Marketing at YALLO Group, a Gulf-focused enterprise technology firm. Raphy works alongside founder/CEO Sumeet Goenka and a small team including Rohit Raj (AI intern), Tanzil (GTM and digital marketing), Anil (web developer/campaigns), and Rafi (web and development lead). YALLO's core offering centers on enterprise software staffing and an emerging "build not buy" AI platform called saasinator.

**Personal context**

No personal context outside of work has been shared across conversations.

**Top of mind**

Raphy is actively running a structured QA testing sprint for the saasinator AI sales chat agent, using a copy-paste loop methodology with three testers (Rohit on Claude, Tanzil on ChatGPT, Anil on Gemini) evaluating the agent against Gulf enterprise buyer personas. A critical gap identified across all test runs is the agent's failure to answer diagnostic pricing and timeline questions, and weak RAG retrieval that surfaces irrelevant vertical content. An aggregate report is due imminently. Raphy also recently stress-tested the agent with a skeptical UAE government persona (VP of Operations archetype) to evaluate its qualification flow and data sovereignty handling.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

Raphy led an extensive rebuild of the saasinator.ai website (v3, Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind 4, Neon/Postgres, pnpm monorepo), repositioning the brand around a "Just In Time Software" framework with three service pillars: LIBERATE, REFORGE, and IGNITE. Major deliverables included a brand book, competitor research guide, chat agent package (packages/chat-agent/), diagnostic page with Calendly embed, and a coming-soon landing page deployed to GitHub Pages via GoDaddy DNS. A saasinator agent test framework was also built as a comprehensive HTML document covering 20 Gulf enterprise buyer personas, a bug taxonomy (P0–P3), scorecard templates, and a Notion-based submission workflow. A separate internal YALLO marketing induction document was created for new team joiners, embedding brand guidelines, YALLO's key metrics (72-hour delivery, 1,000+ engineers placed, tier-1 clients), Canva brand kit details, and an LLM prompt library. Raphy also diagnosed and worked toward resolving a Caddy/Cloudflare reverse proxy issue causing redirect loops on yallo.co's Volcanic-powered careers pages.

*Earlier context*

Raphy explored building a sprint planning web application supporting both human and AI agent team members, with a simulated expert roundtable (React/Next.js + Supabase architecture) to design the data model and tech stack. A Gamma presentation was generated from the dev.saasinator.ai site content covering the platform's AI Factory methodology, 70-20-10 delivery model, and enterprise personas. A standup/onboarding transcript for the saasinator team was summarized, capturing early decisions around Next.js adoption, the ACDC methodology, and product messaging direction.

*Long-term background*

Earlier work included building a UI prototype for an IT Consultant Management system (React, Tailwind, NocoDB considerations) covering HR joiners/leavers workflows, contractor profiles, and onboarding tracking — likely connected to YALLO's staffing business. An Outlook for Mac email extraction project for sumeet.goenka@yallo.co was attempted but abandoned due to platform limitations.

project_memories.019e67fa-5791-75b8-b6fa-973935886127:
**Purpose & context**

Raphy Varghese is Head of Marketing & Growth (also co-founder/product owner) at Yallo Group, leading the GTM strategy and build of **Saasinator 2.0** — an AI-native software delivery brand targeting $1B+ revenue enterprises in UAE and KSA. Saasinator is positioned as a services-led AI systems integrator (never a product or platform), with the brand line: *"Why buy when you can build — Cheaper. Better. Faster."*

**Core service framework (RIL):**
- **REFORGE** — Rebuild legacy .NET/Java/Oracle APEX systems AI-native
- **IGNITE** — Build net-new AI-native applications
- **LIBERATE** — Replace SaaS incumbents (SAP, Oracle, Salesforce, Workday, Blue Yonder, ServiceNow) with owned AI-native equivalents

**Delivery methodology:** AC/DC (Agent Centric Development Cycle) — five AI lenses, nine stages, eight quality gates. Governed by the **Glass Factory** model (board-grade auditability). Full AC/DC methodology is not to be published externally.

**Proof point:** Yallo Intelligence Hub — 35 screens, 8 weeks, one human directing five AI lenses, replacing $6M/year in SaaS.

**Target audience:** CIO/CTO/CFO at 1,000+ employee enterprises. **Industries:** Retail, BFSI, Healthcare, Public Sector, Manufacturing. **Geographies:** Middle East first, then India, then UK/Europe.

**Key people:**
- **Sumeet Goenka** — Founder & CEO; background includes Group Chief Architect roles at Richemont, Landmark Group, Alshaya Group
- **Grid Birthuvi** — Senior Director
- **Geordee George N** — CTO Advisory
- **Internal team:** Anil Pooniya (Track 1: website copy & campaigns), Tanzilul Ahmed (Track 2: GTM & Apollo), Rohitraj Venkata (Track 3: build & engineering), Benson (design & video)

**Hard content rules:**
- No pricing ever on the website
- "saasinator" always lowercase
- No unverified statistics
- AC/DC methodology not published externally
- No AI-generated insights content — voice recorded by authors, transcribed by Anil only
- AC/DC definition block not shown on IGNITE or LIBERATE pages

**Launch milestones:** June 15 soft launch (saasinator.ai goes live); June 29 hard launch at Global AI Show Riyadh (also referenced as ~July 1, 2026).

---

**Current state**

- Website build is active: full content architecture, copy, and page components are in production. Self-contained HTML files (all CSS/JS inline) are the confirmed output format.
- Deployment target is **DigitalOcean App Platform**; the `saasinator-v2` Next.js project (GitHub org: `yallogroup`) was mid-deployment at last session, troubleshooting pnpm/Node compatibility issues on the DO build pipeline. The last known blocker was navigating to component-level build command settings in the DO dashboard — deployment had not yet succeeded.
- A 13-question list for Sumeet Goenka was documented (covering logo clearances, case study approvals, partnership status with TCS, Infosys, AccuKnox, Qburst, demo page access, video recording commitment).
- Virtual hires needed immediately: Full-Stack Lead (Nuxt 3), UX Designer (Figma), Product Launch Expert (3-week contract), Scrum Master, QA Tester.

**Database & infrastructure standard (active directives from Raphy):**
- **Database:** Neon (Postgres) — Supabase has been removed from Saasinator and all Yallo products
- **ORM:** Drizzle (Phase 2)
- **Hosting:** DigitalOcean App Platform

---

**On the horizon**

- Hard launch at Global AI Show Riyadh (June 29 / ~July 1, 2026)
- Phase 2 build includes Drizzle ORM integration with Neon Postgres
- Partnerships to confirm: TCS, Infosys, AccuKnox, Qburst
- GTM and Apollo outreach (Tanzilul's track) coming into activation post-launch

---

**Key learnings & principles**

- Raphy works with Notion MCP extensively; reliable patterns established:
  - Fetch pages by full URL; child pages often require separate fetches (multi-step traversal)
  - `notion-search` with specific project terminology is effective when direct URL fetch returns sparse content
  - Page creation uses `notion-create-pages` with parent `page_id`; Saasinator V2 parent page ID: `36c845af-78b1-8087-b214-f39d95e63a3b` (also referenced as `365845af-78b1-8080-810a-c63b6f9d562c` — use most recent reference)
  - Product backlog database ID: `289f044c03dd4764a43dbd2cb81a0613`
  - Schema definition uses plain English field descriptions with SELECT options and color labels — not SQL syntax
  - `notion-create-pages` parent parameter requires `data_source_id` + `type: data_source_id` dict format
- DO App Platform pnpm gotchas: install scripts must be explicitly enabled (`.npmrc`); pnpm version must be pinned in `package.json` engines field and `packageManager` field; Node version pinned via `.node-version` file; build command must install pnpm explicitly (`npm install -g pnpm@9.15.4 && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`)

---

**Approach & patterns**

- Raphy expects **complete, production-ready deliverables built in a single pass** — not iterative fragments. Fragmenting builds across multiple tool calls or retrying wastes tokens and is explicitly unwelcome.
- Claude should **ask before doing, never guess**, discuss reasoning before building, challenge unverified claims, and bring the team into the thinking process.
- Always **address Raphy by name** when seeking clarification.
- Claude project instructions should be directive and behaviour-shaping, not long and descriptive.

---

**Tools & resources**

- **Frontend:** Next.js (saasinator-v2, GitHub: `yallogroup/saasinator-v2`), Nuxt 3 (Full-Stack Lead hire target), Figma (UX)
- **Design system:** Plus Jakarta Sans (800 weight headings, 300 weight body), DM Mono (labels, eyebrows, mono elements); white background; lavender (#7C3AED), blue (#2563EB), teal (#06B6D4) gradient accents; scroll-reveal via IntersectionObserver
- **Database:** Neon (Postgres); ORM: Drizzle (Phase 2)
- **Hosting:** DigitalOcean App Platform (BLR1 region; app ID: `48c7ce4b-3c1f-4e10-8c6b-b48b32f84f24`, component: `saasinator-v2`)
- **Notion MCP** for project management and documentation
- **Apollo** for GTM outreach (Tanzilul's track)
- **Claude Code CLI** for pushing repo changes
project_memories.019e8373-9e00-71d4-99eb-184229b0523b:
**Purpose & context**

Raphy Varghese is Head of Marketing & Growth at YALLO Group (co-founder energy, product owner), based in Bengaluru. Works closely with Sumeet Goenka (Founder & CEO) — strategic decisions are discussed with Sumeet before acting. Raphy's primary AI-assisted focus is the YALLO AI Academy (academy.yallo.co): a B2B enterprise AI training LMS with a B2C certification track targeting GCC and India markets.

**Core business logic:** YALLO already places AI talent into GCC enterprises. The Academy creates the talent supply pipeline — training professionals, certifying them under the YALLO credential, and placing them into enterprise clients YALLO already has relationships with. Competitive advantage: simultaneous ownership of training credibility, enterprise relationships, and an active placement pipeline.

**Team:**
- Sumeet Goenka — Founder & CEO, content vision, enterprise pipeline, brand
- Anay Goenka — Frontend developer (Sumeet's son)
- Tapan — Trainer & Content Lead, QA owner
- Niharika — talent_viewer role (view-only; cannot enable opted_in on behalf of learners)
- Abhinav — Commercial Lead, talent_viewer role (view-only)
- Anil — Marketing & Growth, demand gen (reports to Raphy)
- Tanzil — Marketing & Growth, content/social (reports to Raphy; drafts content that Raphy approves before publishing)
- Aditya — AI developer (name kept out of team-facing documents; referred to impersonally)
- 10 Claude Code specialist agents: @Marcus (Lead Architect), @David (DB Architect), @Aria (AI Engineer), @Kai (API Architect), @Nina (QA & Security), @Layla (UX Lead), @Rohan (LMS Specialist), @Shreya (Scrum Lead), @Elena (Claude Code Coach), @Michael (Market Analyst)

---

**Current state**

- **49 LMS screens complete** across all roles and modules. Screens are live and linked on a master `index.html` navigator at `saas.doo.ee/lms_screen_dev_brief_v5/` with real-time search (⌘K), role filter chips, and sticky stats banner.
- **Folder structure confirmed:** `Login_Profile/` (S-01, S-02), `Learner/` (S-03–S-20), `Builder/` (S-21, S-22, S-23, S-27), `tutor/` (T-01–T-05), `Admin/` (A-01–A-05), `HR_dashboard/` (S-34–S-38), `Talent/` (U-01–U-05), `AD_DEV_screens/` (AD-01–AD-05).
- **Key file notes:** S-01 has double underscore (`S01__login_register.html`) — preserved in all links. S-03 is in `Learner/` not `Login_Profile/`. Community screens (S-21–S-23) are in `Builder/`.
- Two stale duplicate files identified for deletion: `Ad05.html` (in AD_DEV_screens/) and `U03_ai_skill_path.html` (uploaded to Builder/ by mistake).
- **AD-01–AD-05** (Aditya's placeholder screens) rebuilt in white (#fff) + blue (#2563EB) design system. Video stage in AD-05 (Course Player) intentionally retains black background for the video element only.
- **Architecture documentation:** 7 production HTML tabs delivered (Architecture Brief v4, DB Schema, Sprint Backlog, Execution Guide, .claude/ Structure, API Layer, Team docs). v4 Architecture Brief includes 6 state machines (enrolment lifecycle, quiz attempt tamper-proof, certificate lifecycle, course/lesson publish, talent deployment pipeline, AI tutor session agentic flow).
- **Stack (golden path):** Next.js 15 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 + Drizzle ORM + Neon DB + pnpm + Auth.js v5 + Vercel (Next.js app) + DO Node worker (AI tutor streaming + Postgres-backed background jobs via pg-boss). 40 DB tables. 9 sprints, 18 weeks. 4 agentic AI agents planned for Phase 2.
- **PRD work in progress** — business concept framing established; execution/technical discussions kept separate from strategy discussions.
- **The Raphy Method** documented as standalone `Raphy-Method.html` (one-person software factory methodology, 4 AI roles, 5-stage working loop, 8 factory states, SVG diagrams, sticky sidebar nav).

---

**On the horizon**

- Phase 2: 4 agentic AI agents for the Academy
- Sprint execution with Aditya using the Sprint 0 developer start guide
- PRD completion (business framing done; full document pending)
- **Book project:** *The GenZ Manager* — on managing and communicating with Gen Z. Publishing on Notion.

---

**Key learnings & principles**

- Raphy's design direction to Aditya is prescriptive, not mimetic: "my design should help Aditya to create better features and screen designs" — not copy Aditya's style.
- Strategy and execution are kept strictly separate in conversation; when Claude over-indexes on technical detail during a business discussion, Raphy redirects immediately.
- Implicit approval pattern: moving forward = approved. Corrections come fast, in one sentence, and are definitive.
- Commitlint dropped (no consumer for structured history yet). Husky is local convenience only — CI is the enforcement gate.
- Single approver model: everything gates through Raphy before shipping. Tanzil drafts; Raphy approves. No one enables learner opt-in on Raphy's behalf.

---

**Approach & patterns**

- **Communication:** Short bursts (1–3 lines), heavy typos but precise intent. Claude interprets intent, not literal text. Escalates quickly when output is wrong. Ends sessions with big-picture meta questions.
- **Decision pattern:** Top-down (vision first, details follow). Decides by correction, not pre-specification. Scope expands naturally across sessions. Delegates execution entirely — never writes code or content himself.
- **Working methodology (The Raphy Method):** 8 factory states per project: Define → Team → Screens → DB → API → Execution → Sprints → Ship. Documentation-first (brief before code). Human+AI hybrid team with named specialist agents. Visual review, not line-by-line reading.
- **Output preferences:** Dark luxury HTML (dark background, gold `#D4A843` accent, DM Mono labels). Visual over textual. Tabs/cards over scrolling prose. Architecture diagrams with lines and arrows. Colour-coded (gold = decisions, green = confirmed, red = warnings). One self-contained file per deliverable. Professional footer with version + date.
- **Book writing style (predicted):** Short chapters (one idea per page), practical over academic, first-person direct voice, GCC context throughout, visual chapter openers, named frameworks with acronyms, no fluff, operator credibility framing (what he has actually done, not theory).

---

**Tools & resources**

- **Live screen server:** `saas.doo.ee/lms_screen_dev_brief_v5/` (all 49 screens + master navigator)
- **Stack:** Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, Drizzle ORM, Neon DB (pgvector), Auth.js v5, Vercel, Digital Ocean (Node worker, pg-boss), pnpm
- **Claude Code:** 10 named specialist agents active
- **Book publishing:** Notion
- **Methodology doc:** `Raphy-Method.html`
project_memories.019c512f-acc7-702c-978b-3c125d627085:
**Purpose & context**

Saasinator is Head of Marketing & Growth at YALLO Group (yallo.co), a specialist retail tech talent and enterprise capability firm with offices in Dubai, Bengaluru, and the UK, founded by Sumeet Goenka (former Group Chief Architect at Richemont and Landmark Group; Gartner Peer Community Ambassador). YALLO operates across several verticals: specialist tech talent delivery, technology strategy and enterprise architecture consulting, managed IT services, and a nascent AI Training vertical (YALLO AI Academy) targeting GCC and India markets.

Saasinator works across complex, multi-threaded B2B growth and marketing campaigns simultaneously — typically involving outbound sequencing, landing pages, account research, partnership GTM, and content — and often produces these within single working sessions. Key YALLO colleagues referenced:
- **Sumeet Goenka** – Founder/CEO, primary executive voice and calendar CTA owner
- **Raphy Varghese** – Head of Marketing & Growth (Saasinator's role)
- **Aradhana** – Business Development (calls, WhatsApp follow-ups)
- **Abhinav Kapoor** – Director, Growth & Key Accounts
- **Priyanka Mohan** – BDE, executes Apollo sequences
- **Niharika Patir** – Head of Talent & Operations

---

**Current state**

Several active campaigns and partnership tracks are in motion:

**ANSR sub-vendor campaign:** YALLO is pursuing a sub-vendor partnership with ANSR Global Corporation for niche retail tech talent (India and UAE). A full campaign has been built including a landing page, six-email outreach sequence, Notion campaign playbook, and contact-level drafts. A warm follow-up thread with Arathi Prabhu (Director, TA at ANSR) is active, with touchpoints across email (Raphy) and WhatsApp (Aradhana). The calendar CTA across all assets points to `https://go.yallo.co/sumeet_ansr_20min` / `sumeet.goenka@yallo.co`.

**AccuKnox partnership:** YALLO has signed as GCC reseller and implementation partner for AccuKnox (cloud-native security/CNAPP), targeting UAE and KSA first, with Retail and BFSI as priority verticals. Revenue model is 50/50 on subscription ARR, 100% professional services to YALLO. Key AccuKnox contacts: Raj Panchapakesan (Global Head of BD & Partner Ecosystem) and Emre Kulali (EMEA Partnerships). Deliverables produced include a board-ready deck, 24-page GTM PDF, internal strategy landing page, and a prospect-facing conversion page. Flagged risk: AccuKnox lacks SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certifications.

**Radwell warm-up campaign:** A five-email sequence targeting global Radwell contacts to generate referrals to their India (Bengaluru) hiring team, without referencing the original introduction or lululemon. Tone: industrial domain practitioner, not market intelligence provider. Notion-formatted output produced.

**YALLO AI Academy:** Business model, brand positioning, three-tier service portfolio, proprietary YAMI (YALLO AI Maturity Index) framework, and revenue projections designed. No active campaign execution noted yet.

**Insight Cosmetics:** Assessed as SME/contingency-only account; strategic redirect recommended toward Reliance Retail's beauty vertical (Tira/RCPL).

---

**On the horizon**

- Continued ANSR campaign execution and follow-up cadence management
- AccuKnox GTM activation in UAE/KSA markets
- YALLO AI Academy go-to-market launch
- Book project: *"The GenZ Manager"* by Raphy Varghese — planned on Notion; core thesis is that GenZ requires different *communication*, not different *management*. A SKILL.md author pattern file was created for use in future writing sessions. Additional books on ABM, talent, and growth marketing are possible.
- lululemon specialist roles (SFCC, Snowflake, performance engineering) remain an active talent delivery context tied to the ANSR campaign

---

**Key learnings & principles**

**YALLO positioning rules (non-negotiable):**
- ANSR (and similar partners/clients) must always be framed as the market leader and expert; YALLO is a complementary specialist arm walking *alongside*, never a vendor correcting a weakness or filling a gap
- Banned language: "blind spot," "gap," "struggle," "fix," "rescue," "staffing," and anything that implies the partner is incapable
- This applies to all outbound sequences, landing pages, and partner-facing content

**Content defaults:**
- Blog posts: general YALLO brand voice unless a named individual voice is explicitly requested; keep concise (~500 words), not long-form
- Prospect-facing pages: conversion-only (problem framing, solution, CTA) — no internal strategy data, persona cards, or account lists
- Internal strategy docs and prospect pages are distinct artifacts and must not be conflated

**Tone calibration:**
- Outbound warm-up sequences: no generic market insight claims, no credential-heavy pitches; position as expert practitioners
- Meeting asks withheld until late in sequences (e.g., Email 5 of 5)
- Industrial/domain language used credibly when the sector demands it (e.g., MRO, PLC for Radwell)

**Account qualification:**
- SME accounts with low IT/director-level hiring demand → contingency-only; redirect strategic energy to parent entities or larger adjacent players

---

**Approach & patterns**

- **Communication style:** Saasinator types informally in lowercase with abbreviations and frequent typos; intent is always clear. Directional decisions are made before refinement is requested. Prefers short punchy blocks with headers and emoji over prose paragraphs.
- **Work style:** Runs multiple campaigns and workstreams simultaneously within single sessions; expects Claude to context-switch fluidly
- **Success metrics:** Replies booked and meetings generated — not vanity metrics or open rates
- **Iteration pattern:** Provides pointed corrections mid-task ("don't say that," "make it general," "too salesy"); expects fast incorporation without re-explanation
- **Output format preferences:** Notion-compatible block formatting for playbooks and sequences; HTML for landing pages; pptxgenjs for decks; ReportLab for PDFs
- **Six Thinking Hats framework** has been used for tone analysis and systematic correction of positioning language across email sequences

---

**Tools & resources**

- **Apollo** – outbound email sequencing execution (Priyanka runs sequences)
- **Notion** – campaign playbooks, email sequences, book writing
- **pptxgenjs** – PowerPoint deck generation
- **ReportLab** – PDF report generation
- **HTML** – landing pages (prospect-facing and internal)
- **Canva** – presentation embeds referenced in landing pages
- **go.yallo.co** – short link domain for CTAs
- **yallo.co** – primary brand domain; blog content published here
- **AccuKnox resource links:** accuknox.com/comparisons, /case-studies, /information
- File system conventions (for tool-enabled sessions): transcripts at `/mnt/transcripts/`, outputs at `/mnt/user-data/outputs/`
project_memories.019eb567-6bad-776a-a624-8eee75cbbc1b:
**Purpose & context**

Saasinator is building **saasinator.ai** — a B2B lead generation chat agent delivered as a floating website widget. The core value proposition is converting anonymous visitors into qualified leads booked on a fixed-fee diagnostic call. The project sits at the intersection of sales automation, conversational AI, and SaaS product development.

**Current state**

The agent architecture is defined and under active testing:

- **State machine**: Six-stage flow — GREETING → QUALIFYING → CAPTURING → EDUCATING → CONVERTING → HANDOFF
- **Persona**: Modeled on a senior enterprise software executive
- **Engagement mechanics**: Quick-reply chips, inactivity nudges, email validation logic, RAG-based content retrieval
- **Tech stack**: Claude Haiku 4.5 (primary LLM), Groq Llama 3.3 70B (fallback), OpenAI embeddings, Neon Postgres, Vue frontend

Live persona stress-testing is underway. Completed tests include:
- A cautious government procurement buyer (data sovereignty constraints, no direct budget authority)
- An urgent retail CIO (Salesforce replacement under CEO pressure, 90-day deadline) — this persona successfully progressed through qualifying, email capture, and booking intent within four turns

Multiple build-level issues and test sessions (Test A–E) have been tracked and evaluated against specific buyer personas.

**On the horizon**

- Resolving outstanding build-level issues surfaced across test sessions
- Continued persona stress-testing across buyer types, likely expanding beyond the two completed
- Potential refinement of conversion and handoff stage behavior based on test findings

**Key learnings & principles**

- The agent needs to handle buyers with constrained authority or indirect budget control (e.g., government procurement) differently from high-urgency decision-makers
- A four-turn path to booking intent is achievable with the right persona alignment — the retail CIO test confirmed the capture and conversion flow can work end-to-end
- Competitor landscape has been mapped (Piper, Drift, Intercom Fin, Conversica, HubSpot Breeze) and open-source alternatives reviewed (Typebot, Botpress, Rasa, Chatwoot, OpenAssistantGPT), informing positioning decisions

**Approach & patterns**

- **Output format preference**: Strong preference for clean markdown tables over prose or bullet points — Saasinator has explicitly requested reformatting when output didn't match this style; default to tables when presenting comparative or structured data
- **Testing methodology**: Structured test sessions with named runs (Test A–E), evaluated against specific buyer personas with defined constraints and urgency profiles
- **Stress-testing style**: Saasinator plays buyer personas directly in live chat tests, providing realistic fictional details to probe edge cases in the capture and conversion flow
